executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 Canadian organizations publicly listed as victims in the past 48 hours across all economic sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (consumer goods), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 22 victims, followed by Play (11), Safepay (11), and Clop (11). Critical infrastructure sectors hit include healthcare, education, public sector entities, and financial services—suggesting coordinated or opportunistic mass exploitation rather than isolated incidents.

items.name: Qilin (Agenda)
items.targets: 22 Canadian organizations including Canada Goose, Eastern Townships School Board, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Waterloo Information Systems, Workers Health & Safety Centre, and multiple SMEs across transportation, technology, construction, energy, and professional services sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - double extortion ransomware with data leak site

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Makivik, Security ONE Alarm Systems, and manufacturing/financial services firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - known for targeting mid-size enterprises

items.name: Safepay
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Teccart (education), multiple construction, manufacturing, agriculture firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

items.name: Clop (TA505)
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including law firms (SMT Lawyers), technology providers (Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions), energy (East Plats), hospitality, and business services
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - known for exploiting file transfer vulnerabilities and mass exploitation campaigns

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Labtician Ophthalmics (healthcare), Groupe Caddac (construction), ControlGMC (manufacturing), Colliers
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - newer group targeting professional services

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Phoenix Labs (technology), CCR Solutions, Emond Publishing (education), McKee-Pownall Equine Services
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562 (Impair Defenses)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health (critical public health infrastructure), KTL Group, Trucash (financial services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - targeting public sector and financial services

items.name: IncRansom
items.targets: 8 Canadian organizations across construction, healthcare (Bison Family Medical), education (Bais Yaakov), manufacturing, financial services
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian organizations including Active Green + Ross (automotive retail chain), Morison Insurance Brokers, Kids & Company (childcare services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing)

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Solutions Extreme Technology, Eeyou Communications Network (Indigenous telecommunications provider)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing)

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company - database leaked (major Canadian financial services breach)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - confirmed data leak published

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Inland Audio Visual (business services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1133 (External Remote Services) - known for exploiting VPN vulnerabilities

items.name: Medusa
items.targets: MRC de Maskinongé district government (Quebec regional county municipality)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - targeting municipal governments

items.name: Worldleaks
items.targets: Municipality of North Perth (Ontario municipal government)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing) - targeting Canadian municipal infrastructure

items.name: Payload
items.targets: Q2 Artificial Lift Services (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: Regal Building Materials Ltd, one law firm
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: John O's Foods (agriculture/food production)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Nitrogen
items.targets: Whitfield Welding Inc (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Securotrop
items.targets: Living Realty, Cadman Power Equipment
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: Devman
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Axion50plus (financial services), Ravand (technology), agricultural cooperatives
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: Anubis
items.targets: One law firm in Canada (unnamed)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - targeting legal sector

items.name: CoinbaseCartel
items.targets: Canada Goose (major consumer brand)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: MedusaLocker

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021 (Remote Services) - automated ransomware deployment

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a ransomware crisis with 103 organizations publicly compromised on June 22, 2026, representing all sectors and provinces. Critical impacts include public health (Peterborough), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart), municipal governments (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), financial services (National Money Mart with confirmed data leak, Trucash), and major brands (Canada Goose). No CCCS advisories were published in the reporting period despite the unprecedented attack volume.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (apparel retailer) compromised by CoinbaseCartel and Qilin groups; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail chain) compromised by Sinobi ransomware. Kids & Company (national childcare chain) compromised by Sinobi.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loan/financial services) suffered database leak via Everest ransomware; Trucash (financial services) compromised by Lynx; Morison Insurance Brokers hit by Sinobi; multiple accounting firms affected including Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin), and Reidhurst Nagy (IncRansom). No major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) reported affected.

report_2w.executive_summary: A massive credential leak dubbed 'FortiBleed' has exposed VPN credentials for 73,000 Fortinet devices, prompting an official Canadian government (CCCS) advisory. Splunk Enterprise is under active attack via CVE-2026-20253, a critical unauthenticated remote code execution flaw already being exploited in the wild. Major data breaches this period include Texas state systems leaking 3 million driver's licenses and passports, Amazon's One Medical allegedly breached for 8.8TB of health data, and a Salesforce supply chain attack via the Klue integration affecting multiple cybersecurity firms. International law enforcement (Operation Endgame) has disrupted the SocGholish malware network, seizing 106 servers and cleaning nearly 15,000 infected WordPress sites.

items.name: Evil Corp / SocGholish operators
items.targets: 14,971 WordPress sites globally, distributing FakeUpdates malware
items.ttps: T1189 (Drive-by Compromise), T1204 (User Execution), malware distribution via compromised websites posing as software updates

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: Multiple organizations using Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure
items.ttps: T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1572 (Protocol Tunneling), abusing Microsoft Teams relays to hide C2 traffic for Backdoor.Turn malware

items.name: INC Ransomware
items.targets: 830+ victims since 2023, major RaaS threat in 2026
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), exploits CVE-2023-3519, CVE-2023-48788, CVE-2024-57727, CVE-2025-5777

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Nelson University and others
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion ransomware

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Amazon-owned One Medical (8.8TB data), JCPenney, icsecurity.com
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), large-scale data theft and leak operations

items.name: Icarus Group
items.targets: Klue, Salesforce customers via supply chain attack
items.ttps: T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1528 (Steal Application Access Token), OAuth token abuse via dormant credentials

items.name: Lazarus (North Korea APT)
items.targets: Various organizations (mentioned in threat bulletin)
items.ttps: Advanced persistent threat activities, nation-state espionage

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: Swiss defense contractor Ruag (payment confirmed)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1489 (Service Stop), ransomware operations with ransom payment

items.name: Gamaredon (Russian APT)
items.targets: Ukraine organizations and government
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution), weaponizing CVE-2025-8088 WinRAR vulnerability

items.name: ShadowBytes (Shadowbyt3)
items.targets: Nintendo America via third-party HR platform TinyPulse
items.ttps: T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories), HR data and tax form theft

items.name: PEAR Ransomware
items.targets: Optimum First Mortgage
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

canada_landscape.summary: CCCS issued Alert AL26-014 warning of the FortiBleed credential leak affecting thousands of Fortinet VPN devices globally. A Canadian hacker (Aubrey Cottle) pleaded guilty to cyberattack charges against the Texas Republican website, linked to hacktivist group Anonymous. Newfoundland and Labrador health authority employees received suspicious emails, highlighting ongoing phishing threats in provincial healthcare.
